page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-384-metal is a bare-metal server instance belonging to the c4d server family. It is equipped with 384 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 CPU architecture. The system features 3072.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This bare-metal configuration does not include local storage, meaning all storage must be provisioned externally. There are no integrated GPUs, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given its massive memory capacity and high dedicated core count, this server offers high resource density for enterprise-scale workloads. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it suitable for memory-intensive applications, large-scale databases, in-memory analytics, and heavy enterprise workloads that require dedicated physical hardware without virtualization overhead.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-384-metal is a bare-metal server configuration within the c4d family, designed for high-density memory and compute workloads. It features 384 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture and is equipped with 3072.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated graphics processing units, requiring external storage attachment for data persistence. The absence of virtualization overhead on this bare-metal platform, combined with its substantial memory footprint, makes it highly suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and intensive data processing workloads that demand dedicated physical hardware resources.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-384-metal is a bare-metal server featuring 384 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 3072 GB of RAM, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal platform featuring three terabytes of memory and three hundred eighty-four dedicated cores for massive enterprise workloads.

No local storage or integrated GPUs included
Designed for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise workloads
